test_results.output: 77 passed, 0 failed. Local audit workflow tests passed.

findings.title: Command injection via unquoted DEPLOY_CMD in fix-bugs.sh
findings.description: The deploy command variable is expanded without quoting ($DEPLOY_CMD) and the regex character allowlist does not block spaces, which means multi-word commands with shell metacharacters that pass the filter can still trigger word-splitting and globbing. The shellcheck SC2086 disable comment confirms the intent to skip this warning.
findings.impact: An attacker who controls deploy_command in config could execute arbitrary shell commands in the target repo directory.
findings.fix_suggestion: Split DEPLOY_CMD into an array at config parse time and invoke as "${deploy_cmd_array[@]}", or validate against a strict allowlist of known commands.

findings.title: No Content-Length limit in API server allows memory exhaustion
findings.description: The _read_body method in the API server converts Content-Length to int and reads that many bytes with no upper bound. A malicious client can send Content-Length: 999999999999 to force a massive memory allocation.
findings.impact: Denial of service via memory exhaustion on the API server.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add MAX_CONTENT_LENGTH = 1_000_000 and reject requests exceeding it with a 413 response before reading the body.

findings.title: Workflow template injection via unsanitized shell commands
findings.description: scaffold-github-workflows.py uses string .replace() to insert lint_command, test_command, and build_command from targets config directly into GitHub Actions YAML. These values are not validated or escaped, allowing arbitrary command injection into generated CI workflows.
findings.evidence:
content.replace("__LINT_COMMAND__", target.get("lint_command", "echo 'set lint command'"))
.replace("__TEST_COMMAND__", target.get("test_command", ...))
.replace("__BUILD_COMMAND__", normalize_build_command(target))
findings.impact: Malicious values in targets.yaml could inject arbitrary commands into generated GitHub Actions workflows, leading to supply-chain compromise.
findings.fix_suggestion: Validate that command values match a safe pattern (e.g., ^[a-zA-Z0-9 _./:-]+$) before templating. Alternatively, use proper YAML serialization (ruamel.yaml) instead of string replacement.

findings.title: GitHub OIDC role uses wildcard branch condition
findings.description: The OIDC trust policy for the GitHub deploy role uses 'repo:CodyJo/back-office:*' which allows ANY branch, tag, environment, or pull request to assume the AWS deployment role.
findings.impact: A compromised feature branch or pull request could assume the deploy role and push malicious content to the production S3 bucket and invalidate the CloudFront cache.
findings.fix_suggestion: Restrict to main branch only: "repo:CodyJo/back-office:ref:refs/heads/main"

findings.title: XSS via unescaped innerHTML in product selector
findings.description: In index.html, product names from orgData are inserted into innerHTML without HTML escaping. An esc() function exists in the same file but is not used here. If product.name contains HTML/JS, it will execute.
findings.impact: Stored XSS if malicious product names are injected into the data source, allowing arbitrary code execution in the dashboard viewer's browser.
findings.fix_suggestion: Use the existing esc() function: `<option value="${esc(product.key)}">${esc(product.name)}</option>`

findings.title: API server target path resolution lacks validation
findings.description: The resolve_target() function in api-server.py accepts a site_hint from user input and resolves it to a filesystem path. While there is a path existence check, there is no validation against path traversal or symlink attacks.
findings.impact: An attacker with API access could point scans at arbitrary directories on the filesystem.
findings.fix_suggestion: Validate resolved paths against an allowlist of configured target directories. Use os.path.realpath() to resolve symlinks and verify the result is within allowed paths.

findings.title: Race condition in file locking with truncation
findings.description: The with_run_lock decorator in local_audit_workflow.py uses fcntl.flock but creates the lock file with 'w' mode (truncating it each time). This can cause the lock to be released prematurely if another process opens the file between truncation and flock acquisition.
findings.impact: Concurrent audit runs could corrupt results files or produce inconsistent state.
findings.fix_suggestion: Open the lock file with 'a' mode instead of 'w' to avoid truncating. Consider writing the PID for stale lock detection.

findings.title: Path traversal risk from unsanitized REPO_NAME in shell scripts
findings.description: Multiple agent scripts derive REPO_NAME from basename of the user-supplied TARGET_REPO path and use it to construct results directory paths without validating the characters.
findings.impact: Directory traversal could cause results to be written to unintended locations if REPO_NAME contains special characters.
findings.fix_suggestion: Validate REPO_NAME matches ^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$ before using in paths.

findings.title: Subprocess errors silently swallowed in API server agent runner
findings.description: The _run() function inside run_agent() uses try/finally without except, meaning subprocess failures are silently discarded. The job is removed from running_jobs but no error is recorded.
findings.impact: Failed scans appear to complete successfully. Users have no visibility into scan failures via the API.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add except clause to log errors, store failure status in a shared dict, and expose error state via the /api/jobs endpoint.

findings.title: Symlink following in workflow file glob
findings.description: generate-delivery-data.py uses Path.glob() to list workflow files in target repos. glob() follows symlinks by default, so a malicious target repo could create symlinks to sensitive files on the system.
findings.impact: Information disclosure — a crafted target repo could cause the system to read arbitrary YAML files from the filesystem.
findings.fix_suggestion: Check that resolved paths (file.resolve()) are still within workflow_dir before reading. Filter out symlinks with file.is_symlink().

findings.title: CSP allows unsafe-inline for scripts across all dashboards
findings.description: All dashboard HTML files set Content-Security-Policy with script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline', which significantly weakens XSS protection since inline scripts are allowed.
findings.impact: Any XSS vulnerability (like FIND-005) can execute arbitrary inline scripts because the CSP does not block them.
findings.fix_suggestion: Move inline scripts to external .js files and remove 'unsafe-inline' from CSP, or use nonce-based CSP.

findings.title: Admin API key stored in sessionStorage
findings.description: backoffice.html stores the admin API key in sessionStorage, which is accessible to any script running in the same origin.
findings.impact: If any XSS vulnerability exists on the same origin, the stored API key can be exfiltrated.
findings.fix_suggestion: Keep the key only in a JavaScript closure variable (in-memory), not in sessionStorage. Require re-entry on page refresh.

findings.title: Full environment propagated to subprocess in local_audit_workflow.py
findings.description: When launching subprocess for dashboard refresh, the entire os.environ is spread into the child process environment. This could leak sensitive environment variables (AWS keys, tokens) to child scripts.
findings.impact: Sensitive credentials in the parent environment are exposed to all child processes.
findings.fix_suggestion: Build a minimal allowlist of environment variables (PATH, HOME, LANG) instead of spreading the full os.environ.

findings.title: No schema validation on loaded JSON results
findings.description: Multiple scripts (aggregate-results.py, api-server.py, local_audit_workflow.py) load JSON from the results directory without validating the schema. Malformed or malicious JSON could cause unexpected behavior.
findings.impact: Corrupted or crafted results files could cause dashboard data generation to fail or produce misleading output.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add lightweight schema validation for required fields (summary.total, findings array) before processing.

findings.title: API server lacks rate limiting
findings.description: The dashboard API server has no rate limiting on any endpoints. An attacker could repeatedly trigger expensive scan operations or brute-force the API key.
findings.impact: Resource exhaustion via rapid scan requests or API key brute-force.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add a simple per-IP rate limiter (e.g., token bucket in a dict keyed by client IP) to the request handler. Limit scan triggers to 1 per minute per department.

findings.title: Agent scripts don't validate TARGET is an absolute path
findings.description: All agent scripts accept TARGET_REPO as an argument and validate it's a git directory, but don't verify it's an absolute path. Relative paths could resolve differently depending on the working directory.
findings.impact: Relative paths could cause scans to target unintended directories depending on invocation context.
findings.fix_suggestion: Normalize the path early: TARGET_REPO="$(cd "$1" && pwd)" or check that it starts with /.

findings.title: Inline Python blocks in shell scripts are fragile and hard to audit
findings.description: Multiple shell scripts (sync-dashboard.sh, quick-sync.sh, job-status.sh) contain large inline Python blocks executed via python3 -c. These are difficult to test, lint, and audit for security issues.
findings.evidence: python3 -c "import json, sys; ..." (multi-line inline Python in bash)
findings.impact: Maintenance burden and increased risk of undetected bugs in untestable inline code.
findings.fix_suggestion: Extract inline Python blocks to standalone .py scripts that can be linted, tested, and version-controlled independently.

findings.title: Terraform state stored locally instead of remote encrypted backend
findings.description: terraform.tfstate is stored locally. While it is gitignored, local state files risk being lost, leaked, or corrupted. The state contains AWS account IDs and resource ARNs.
findings.evidence: Local terraform.tfstate file exists with AWS account ID and resource ARNs
findings.impact: State file loss could make Terraform unable to manage existing infrastructure. Leaked state reveals infrastructure details.
findings.fix_suggestion: Migrate to S3 backend with encryption and DynamoDB locking: terraform { backend "s3" { ... } }

findings.title: Silent flock failure in job-status.sh
findings.description: The flock command failure is suppressed with '|| true', meaning concurrent writes to the jobs JSON can occur if locking fails.
findings.impact: Corrupted jobs.json if multiple scans update status simultaneously.
findings.fix_suggestion: Remove '|| true' and fail explicitly if the lock cannot be acquired within the timeout.

findings.title: Missing error handling for int() conversion in scoring
findings.description: qa_score_from_summary() calls int() on dict values that may not be numeric, which would raise ValueError.
findings.impact: Unhandled exception if findings JSON contains non-numeric severity counts.
findings.fix_suggestion: Wrap in try/except ValueError or use a safe_int helper that defaults to 0.

findings.title: Missing alt attribute on dynamic images in backoffice.html
findings.description: Dynamically generated img elements for product thumbnails lack alt attributes, violating WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility requirements.
findings.impact: Screen readers cannot describe the images. Fails WCAG 2.1 AA Perceivable principle.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add alt="${esc(p.name || 'Product thumbnail')}" to the img tag.

findings.title: Hardcoded department mappings duplicated across scripts
findings.description: Department name-to-file mappings are duplicated in quick-sync.sh, job-status.sh, api-server.py, and local_audit_workflow.py. Adding a new department requires updating all locations.
findings.evidence: Department case/map duplicated in quick-sync.sh, job-status.sh, api-server.py
findings.impact: Maintenance burden and risk of inconsistency when adding departments.
findings.fix_suggestion: Extract department registry to a shared config file (e.g., departments.yaml) and read it at runtime.
